textPlain: Because it did not top the open source leaderboard on any benchmark, except the agent one maybe. The hosted versions are not currently cheaper or faster than the other open source models, either.

From the perspective of China, it probably makes sense to try and train on local chips and try to dethrone Nvidia. I guess this means PRC thinks AGI isnt around the corner and they can catch up on hardware.
It also seems reasonable for me to think AGI isn't around the corner given how much current AI technology has failed in all fronts to deliver something both general and intelligent.
They're already making the robots to run these models, which are the complements they are commoditizing.
